data.body: PASS 1 (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): CLEAN. This is a caching-infrastructure plan at the runner seam, not a plan that defines new LLM schemas/enums/sub-agents/protocols. Tool-verified: the SPIKE question ("does anthropic_cache_instructions cache system_prompt= or only instructions=?") is resolved in-code (runner.py:213-214 cites the anthropic.py:1611-1616 no-instruction-parts branch that caches the system-prompt block directly); the RunUsage field-name pin is concrete and correct (_extract_usage at runner.py:394-418 documents the library's NORMALIZATION of cache_read_input_tokens→cache_read_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens→cache_write_tokens at usage.py:194-200). The plan's fallback for the failed/incomplete sub-step (spike resolving "instructions-only") is explicitly specified (route stable plan through instructions=, else user-message CachePoint, deferred). No undefined-but-referenced enum/protocol, no ambiguous counter placement, no instruction-locality antipattern. No T8 findings.
